title: Nephrodium ptarmica
taxonomy: Nephrodium ptarmica is actually now classified as a synonym of Blechnum ptarmicum. It belongs to the family Blechnaceae. This species is a type of fern and is native to various parts of the world, including South America, Australia, and New Zealand. Blechnum ptarmicum is a terrestrial fern that grows in shaded, humid areas. It is often found in ravines, gorges, and other areas with high moisture levels.
uses: Nephrodium ptarmica, also known as a fern, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant has been studied for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce pain and swelling. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses of Nephrodium ptarmica.

intro:
There is no plant called Nephrodium ptarmica. 

However, I assume you meant to ask about Nephrolepis exaltata, which was previously classified under the genus Nephrodium, or possibly Nephrolepis ptarmica, but I found no information for the latter.

Here is a paragraph for Nephrolepis exaltata:

Nephrolepis exaltata, also known as Boston fern or ladder fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as bronchitis, fever, and menstrual cramps. The bioactive constituents of Nephrolepis exaltata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Nephrolepis exaltata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
